markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Extract Rust modules, structs, enums, traits, impls, functions, tests, imports, doc comments, and spans. This MT belongs to CodeIndexingAndNavigation. Index code structure and expose precise backend navigation for agents.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Code navigation must use Handshake-managed parsers/adapters and store spans/citations in PostgreSQL.
Tree-sitter is the first parser family; LSP/SCIP-style precision is a Handshake-owned import/projection path, not an outside service requirement.
scope.expected_behavior: RustSymbolExtractor leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: RustSymbolExtractor is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.implementation_evidence: wtc-kernel-009 feat/WP-KERNEL-009 commits 5d6490b0 (compiling foundation) + 79e1a7e4 (runtime proof); 43 lib + 11 integration + 1 nav-API tests green on real PostgreSQL under runtime-full,test-utils. Found+fixed impl-Type/struct-Type entity collision. Pending adversarial code review.

concerns: RESIDUAL entity_key collision beyond the one the delegate fixed. The fix disambiguates struct-Type vs impl-Type (symbols.rs:231 path_name='impl {name}'), but does NOT disambiguate TWO impl blocks of the SAME type. `impl A {}` + `impl Trait for A {}`, or `impl T1 for A {}` + `impl T2 for A {}`, both yield symbol_path 'impl A' (scope_name_for returns the `type` field 'A' for both), producing identical entity_key `rust:path#impl A`. The upsert ON CONFLICT (workspace_id,entity_kind,entity_key) at knowledge.rs:3336 then SILENTLY MERGES the two distinct impl entities into one (display_name/provenance overwritten by last writer; both spans attach to survivor). Distinct symbols collapse - the same failure class the delegate claimed closed; only the struct-vs-impl variant was closed.

Method-name collision across impl blocks of the same type: two `impl A` blocks each defining `fn run` both scope to `A::run` (scope uses the impl `type`, not the trait), so a trait method and an inherent method of the same name on the same type collide on `A::run` and merge.
Generic impls are inconsistently handled: `impl A<u32>` vs `impl A<String>` get distinct keys (type-field text differs) but the COMMON bare repeated `impl A` collides.
evasion_probes: Traced extract_rust (symbols.rs:193-250): SymbolKind::Impl path_name='impl {name}', name=scope_name_for->`type` field. For two impls of type A at module top level, scope_path adds no prefix -> both 'impl A'. The delegate's test rust_extracts_fns_structs_impls_methods_traits_tests covers only a SINGLE impl, so this collision is untested.

Verified #[test]/#[cfg(test)] detection (rust_has_test_attribute) keys on attribute text containing 'test' - a non-test attr like #[contest] or a doc mentioning test would NOT match because it scans attribute_item nodes only; acceptable.
Confirmed free fn vs method of same name do NOT collide (distinct paths).

hardening_closed.note: Adversarial finding fixed + real-PG/unit test green (knowledge_code_index_tests 13 + nav_api 2 + 59 unit, 0 fail).

evidence: Hardened b0266f35: entity_key disambiguation: trait-impl methods tagged as:Trait, impls tagged, collision ordinals; distinct symbols no longer merge.

findings.title: Collision ordinal is positional, so entity_key (and derived relationship_id) churns when impl blocks / duplicates are added or reordered
findings.evidence: assign_collision_ordinals (symbols.rs:140-168) assigns dup0/dup1 by extraction order to same-(symbol_path,disambiguator) symbols. Two impl blocks of the same type no longer silently merge (V1 finding closed), but adding/removing/reordering an impl re-numbers the others, changing their entity_key and any relationship_id derived from it.
findings.fix: Derive the ordinal from a stable intra-file property (e.g. byte-span order is already stable, but insertion above still shifts it) or include a content/signature hash so the discriminator is edit-stable.

adversarial_review_v2.note: VERIFIED FIXED from V1 (b0266f35): the silent two-impl/same-type merge at the upsert is resolved by disambiguator + ordinal (test duplicate_same_kind_declarations_get_ordinal_and_do_not_collide, symbols.rs:823). Residual is positional-ordinal churn, a lesser issue than the merge it replaced.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-098 requires a Rust symbol extractor (fns, structs, enums, traits, impls, methods, modules, consts, type aliases, macros, tests, scopes, spans) that produces stable entity_keys persisted as `symbol` KnowledgeEntities anchored to AST spans in PostgreSQL/EventLedger. The implementation in symbols.rs delivers this, fixes the prior V1 entity_key collision-merge defect, aligns with current Master Spec 2.3.13.11, and is proven end-to-end against real Handshake-managed PostgreSQL.
findings: symbols.rs:256-331 extract_rust covers the full MT-098 kind set: function_item (split into Function/Method/Test via rust_has_test_attribute + rust_is_in_impl_or_trait), struct_item, enum_item, trait_item, impl_item, mod_item, const/static_item, type_item, macro_definition; scope_path + RUST_SCOPE_KINDS build dotted paths (e.g. Widget::render); start/end byte+line spans carried.

PRIOR V1 FAIL (entity_key silent merge) is fixed in commit b0266f35: the `disambiguator` field (symbols.rs:100) + assign_collision_ordinals (symbols.rs:140-165) give distinct keys to inherent vs trait-impl methods (A::run vs A::run~as:T) and to two impl blocks of the same type (impl A~as:T1 / ~as:T2 / ~inherent). Unit tests rust_trait_and_inherent_method_same_name_do_not_collide (symbols.rs:1021) and rust_two_trait_impls_of_same_type_do_not_collide (symbols.rs:1045) assert assert_unique_keys.

Real-PG runtime proof: tests/knowledge_code_index_tests.rs drives the real CodeIndexEngine -> storage::knowledge::KnowledgeStore -> managed PostgreSQL (knowledge_pg_support.rs uses ManagedPostgres::ensure_running, port 5544; header explicitly states NO SQLite/in-memory/mock fallback, SKIPs loudly if PG binaries absent). Lines 377-390 read symbol entities back from PG and assert rust:src/lib.rs#add and the disambiguated rust:src/lib.rs#Widget::render~as:Render; lines 422-438 assert each symbol has an `ast`-kind KnowledgeSpan with line_start>0.

Spec alignment: Master Spec v02.194 2.3.13.11 (02-system-architecture.md:2701-2702) defines KnowledgeEntity as 'a typed symbol ... detected from one or more spans' and KnowledgeSpan including AST ranges as the minimum citeable evidence unit; the impl maps Rust symbols to `symbol` entities anchored to `ast` spans, matching exactly.

Discriminator is non-leaky: api/knowledge_code_nav.rs:797-800 symbol_simple_name strips the `~` discriminator before name/path lookup, so collision-resistance does not break navigation by simple name (test simple_name_extraction, line 882).
Grep of symbols.rs for todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/Unavailable/'not yet wired'/'pending MT-'/placeholder/stub returns no matches: no deferred-live escape on any required path.

Residual (non-blocking, V2 adversarial LOW): assign_collision_ordinals dup ordinals are positional, so adding/reordering duplicate decls re-numbers others' entity_key. This is an edit-stability nit on genuinely-duplicate symbols, not a merge/correctness defect; the prior fail-class (silent merge) is closed.
evidence_checked: git show --stat b0266f35; git log --grep MT-098

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
